Requirement: K1 must be defined in the BAO log-residual target space.
Reason: Prevents mixing raw observables with residual diagnostics.
FailureMode: K2 multiplies an object that is not the scored residual target.

Requirement: K1 must declare whether it is attached to independent CMB-only baseline, same-data sensitivity, or future likelihood-native baseline.
FailureMode: K2 appears viable only because the baseline was selected post-hoc.

Requirement: K1 amplitude normalization must be declared before K2 scoring.
Reason: Prevents residual amplitude fitting from becoming hidden K2 evidence.
FailureMode: K2 amplitude is tuned to the data under the name of K1.

Requirement: K1 amplitude norm must use the same covariance metric as K2 and nulls.
FailureMode: K1 looks small or large only under a different metric.
NextAction: Use public residual covariance for all norms.

Requirement: Same-data best-fit or rd-offset controls cannot define locked K1 amplitude.
Reason: Prevents same-data residual absorption from becoming the no-memory response.
FailureMode: K1 is already fit to the tested BAO vector.
NextAction: Use same-data controls only for sensitivity reporting.

Requirement: Zero residual, constant offset, polynomial controls, and independent baseline residual must be reported with K1.
Reason: Separates finite-memory response from generic smooth or offset behavior.
FailureMode: K2 advantage is indistinguishable from simpler nulls.
NextAction: Require null scorecard before K2 interpretation.
